meta.title: Clinic Analytics Software | LobbyView Dashboards & Reporting | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to clinic analytics. Real-time LobbyView dashboards. Wait time metrics. Provider productivity. No-show tracking. Custom reports.

hero.subhead: See what's actually happening in your practice. Real-time dashboards show patient flow, wait times, and bottlenecks. Historical reports reveal patterns and trends. Data drives improvement.

sections.content:
Most practices operate on intuition. 'I think we're busy.' 'It feels like wait times are long.' 'I guess Dr. Smith sees fewer patients.' Intuition is often wrong.

**What you don't measure, you can't improve:**

Are wait times getting better or worse? Which provider runs behind most? What time of day has the worst bottlenecks? Why do patients no-show?

Without data, you're guessing. With data, you're managing.

**The insight gap:**

EHRs generate data but don't present it usefully. Billing systems track revenue but not operations. Practice management systems show schedules but not flow.

clinIQ connects the dots — from patient arrival through checkout, from scheduling through billing — to show what's actually happening.

**Data-driven improvement:**

Practices using operational analytics see:
- 20-40% reduction in wait times
- 10-15% improvement in provider productivity
- 20-30% reduction in no-show rates
- Significant improvements in patient satisfaction

The improvements come from visibility. Once you see the problem, you can fix it.

LobbyView is clinIQ's real-time operational dashboard. See what's happening right now.

**What LobbyView shows:**

Patient status:
- Who's in the waiting room (and how long)
- Who's in exam rooms (and with whom)
- Who's checking out
- Who's running late or hasn't arrived

Room status:
- Which rooms are occupied, available, or in turnover
- Time in current status
- Assignment by provider

Provider status:
- Who's with patients
- Who's between patients
- Running behind or ahead of schedule

Practice-wide metrics:
- Total patients in building
- Current average wait time
- Bottleneck indicators

**Display options:**

Wall displays: Large screens in clinical areas showing room status and patient queues.

Desktop: Browser-based dashboard for staff workstations.

Mobile: Phone/tablet access for managers on the go.

TV mode: Ambient display for break rooms showing practice-wide metrics.

**Real-time alerts:**

Configurable alerts when thresholds are exceeded:
- 'Waiting room average exceeds 15 minutes'
- 'Room 3 has been in turnover for 10 minutes'
- 'Dr. Jones is 20 minutes behind schedule'

Alerts enable intervention before problems cascade.

Wait times are the #1 driver of patient satisfaction — and dissatisfaction. Measuring them is the first step to improving them.

**Wait time components:**

Waiting room wait: Check-in → rooming
Exam room wait: Rooming → provider entry
Total wait: Check-in → provider entry
Total visit time: Check-in → checkout

Each component tells a different story. Long waiting room waits suggest rooming problems. Long room waits suggest provider delays.

**Analytics available:**

Current metrics:
- Average wait right now
- Longest current wait
- Wait time by patient

Historical metrics:
- Average wait by day of week
- Average wait by time of day
- Average wait by provider
- Average wait by appointment type
- Trend over time (weeks, months)

**Pattern identification:**

Analytics reveal patterns:
- 'Monday mornings average 22-minute waits; Tuesday mornings average 12 minutes'
- 'New patient visits have 8-minute longer waits than follow-ups'
- 'Dr. Smith's patients wait 35% longer than Dr. Jones's'

Patterns suggest interventions:
- Adjust Monday scheduling to reduce volume
- Schedule new patients with buffer time
- Review Dr. Smith's workflow

**Benchmarking:**

How do you compare?
- Excellent: < 10 minutes total wait
- Good: 10-15 minutes
- Average: 15-25 minutes
- Needs improvement: > 25 minutes

clinIQ shows where you stand and tracks improvement over time.

Provider productivity varies significantly — and that variation represents opportunity.

**Productivity metrics:**

Volume metrics:
- Patients seen per day/week/month
- Patients per hour of clinic time
- RVUs generated

Time metrics:
- Average time per patient
- Time per appointment type
- Non-patient time (documentation, admin)

Efficiency metrics:
- Room utilization
- On-time start rate
- Schedule adherence

**Provider comparison:**

Compare providers on key metrics:
- Dr. Smith sees 22 patients/day; Dr. Jones sees 18
- Dr. Smith averages 14 minutes per patient; Dr. Jones averages 18
- Dr. Smith runs 5 minutes behind on average; Dr. Jones runs 15 minutes behind

Comparison isn't about judgment — it's about identifying best practices and improvement opportunities.

**Trend analysis:**

Track over time:
- Is provider X seeing more or fewer patients?
- Are visit times increasing or decreasing?
- Is on-time performance improving?

**Actionable insights:**

Productivity data enables conversations:
- 'You're running 15 minutes behind on average. Let's look at why.'
- 'Your new patient visits take 50 minutes; the target is 45. What's happening?'
- 'You have the highest patients per hour — what's your secret?'

Data removes defensiveness and focuses on improvement.

No-shows cost practices significant revenue. Understanding patterns enables reduction.

**No-show metrics:**

Overall rate:
- Practice-wide no-show percentage
- Trend over time

Segmented rates:
- By provider
- By appointment type
- By day of week
- By time of day
- By patient demographics
- By lead time (days between scheduling and appointment)

**Pattern identification:**

Common patterns:
- Friday afternoons have highest no-show rates
- New patients no-show more than established patients
- Appointments scheduled 4+ weeks out no-show more than those scheduled within 2 weeks
- Patients who don't confirm no-show at 3x the rate of those who confirm

**Predictive indicators:**

clinIQ can flag high no-show risk patients:
- History of prior no-shows
- Didn't confirm appointment
- Long lead time
- Certain appointment types

High-risk patients can be:
- Double-confirmed
- Called day-of
- Overbooked against

**Intervention tracking:**

Measure the impact of no-show reduction efforts:
- Did confirmation texts reduce no-shows?
- Did shorter lead times help?
- Which interventions work?

Operational metrics connect to revenue. Understanding this connection drives financial improvement.

**Capacity metrics:**

Scheduled capacity:
- Appointment slots available
- Slots booked vs. available
- Utilization percentage

Actual throughput:
- Patients actually seen
- No-shows and cancellations
- Same-day fills

Capacity opportunity:
- Empty slots that could have been filled
- Lost revenue from no-shows
- Potential from improved scheduling

**RTM revenue tracking:**

For practices using RTM:
- Patients enrolled
- Patients meeting billing criteria
- Monthly RTM revenue
- Revenue per patient
- Enrollment rate trends

**Procedure metrics:**

For procedural practices:
- Procedures per day/week
- Procedure room utilization
- Turnover time
- Revenue per procedure

**Revenue correlation:**

Connect operational metrics to revenue:
- 'Reducing wait times by 10 minutes correlates with 8% higher patient retention'
- 'Each 1% reduction in no-shows = $X additional revenue'
- 'Each additional patient per day = $Y annual revenue'

Standard dashboards cover common needs. Custom reports address specific questions.

**Report builder:**

Create custom reports by selecting:
- Metrics to include
- Time period
- Filters (provider, location, appointment type)
- Grouping and sorting
- Visualization (table, chart, both)

**Scheduled reports:**

Automate regular reporting:
- Daily morning summary to practice manager
- Weekly productivity report to providers
- Monthly executive summary to owners
- Quarterly trend analysis

Reports delivered via email or available in dashboard.

**Export capabilities:**

Export data for external analysis:
- Excel/CSV export
- PDF reports
- Integration with BI tools

**Common custom reports:**

- Provider comparison scorecard
- Weekly operations summary
- Monthly trend report
- No-show analysis
- Wait time deep dive
- RTM program performance
- Authorization turnaround analysis

Analytics implementation starts immediately — data begins flowing as soon as clinIQ is live.

**Day 1:**

Real-time dashboards work immediately:
- Patient status visible
- Room status visible
- Wait times calculated

No historical data yet, but current operations are visible.

**Week 1-2:**

Patterns begin emerging:
- Initial wait time baselines
- Early productivity patterns
- No-show tracking begins

**Month 1:**

Meaningful historical data:
- Compare week over week
- Identify day-of-week patterns
- Establish benchmarks

**Month 3+:**

Trend analysis possible:
- Are we improving?
- Which interventions worked?
- Where should we focus next?

**Using analytics effectively:**

1. Start with key metrics: Don't try to track everything. Pick 3-5 metrics that matter most.

2. Review regularly: Weekly operations review using dashboards.

3. Set goals: 'Reduce average wait time from 22 to 15 minutes in 3 months.'

4. Track interventions: When you change something, measure the impact.

5. Share results: Make metrics visible to staff. Celebrate improvements.

faqs.q: How quickly do we get data?
faqs.a: Real-time data is available immediately. Historical trends require time to accumulate — meaningful patterns emerge within 2-4 weeks.
Yes. Select which metrics appear, set thresholds for alerts, and create custom views by role. Different users can have different default dashboards.

faqs.q: Does this replace our reporting from the EHR?
faqs.a: clinIQ focuses on operational metrics — patient flow, wait times, productivity. EHR reports typically focus on clinical data. They're complementary, not duplicative.
Yes. Multi-location practices can compare metrics across sites, identify best practices, and standardize operations.

faqs.q: How do you calculate wait times?
faqs.a: Wait times are calculated from actual timestamps — check-in time, rooming time, provider entry time. No estimation or sampling.

faqs.q: Can providers see their own metrics?
faqs.a: Yes, if you want them to. Provider access to their own productivity and wait time data is configurable. Some practices share openly; others limit access.

faqs.q: Do you benchmark against other practices?
faqs.a: We can provide benchmarks based on specialty and practice type. This helps contextualize your performance — are your wait times good or bad compared to peers?

faqs.q: Can we export data for our own analysis?
faqs.a: Yes. Export to Excel/CSV for external analysis. Some practices integrate with BI tools for combined reporting with financial data.

cta.headline: See your practice in real-time
cta.subhead: 15-minute demo. LobbyView dashboards, wait time analytics, provider productivity, custom reports.

testimonial.quote: We thought we knew how the practice was running. The data showed us we were wrong. Wait times were twice what we thought. Now we actually know — and we've cut them in half.
